findings.title: Hardcoded absolute path detected
findings.description: The file .code-analysis/validation/run-comparison.sh contains hardcoded absolute paths (/Users/keven.foster/...) on lines 4 and 6. Hardcoded paths break portability across environments.
findings.recommendation: Replace hardcoded paths with ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} or a relative path derived from the script's own location (e.g., SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)).

findings.title: Unrecognized field in plugin.json: peerDependencies
findings.description: plugin.json contains a 'peerDependencies' field that is not part of the recognized plugin manifest schema (name, version, description, author, keywords, license, homepage, repository, dependencies, hooks, mcp). This may be ignored by Claude Code.
findings.recommendation: Move peerDependencies to package.json or document why this field exists in plugin.json. If Claude Code does not consume it, it adds noise.

findings.title: package.json scoped name diverges from plugin name
findings.description: package.json declares name '@vooban/code-analysis' while plugin.json declares 'code-analysis'. The npm scoped prefix creates a naming inconsistency that could confuse tooling or users.
findings.recommendation: Align naming conventions: either use 'code-analysis' in both files, or document the scoped vs unscoped naming rationale.

findings.title: Description exceeds 200 characters
findings.description: The description field in plugin.json is 297 characters long. Marketplace listings may truncate descriptions beyond 200 characters, resulting in incomplete display.
findings.snippet: "description": "Comprehensive codebase analysis across 4 standard dimensions (structure, quality, security, testing) — produces scored reports with critic validation and focused refactoring plans. v0.8.0 adds token optimizations (split schemas, skip-clean ralph-loop passes, inline scoring, smart model defaults)."
findings.recommendation: Shorten the description to under 200 characters by moving changelog details (v0.8.0 additions) to README.md or a CHANGELOG file.

findings.title: Non-standard top-level directories: docs, templates
findings.description: The plugin contains top-level directories (docs/, templates/) not part of the standard plugin layout (skills/, agents/, hooks/, references/, mcp-servers/, commands/). These directories are not auto-discovered by Claude Code but may serve legitimate purposes (documentation, scaffolding).
findings.recommendation: No action required unless plugin registry enforces strict layout. Consider moving docs content into README.md and templates into references/ if applicable.

findings.title: Plugin uses 117 CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT references across 30 files
findings.description: The plugin makes extensive use of ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for path references. All usages follow the correct braces syntax. No malformed references ($CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT without braces or parent traversal) were found in plugin source files.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. The plugin correctly uses ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} throughout.
